search_terms_used: 5K training physiology performance determinants meta-analysis pubmed
sprint interval training 5km running performance pubmed
neuromuscular factors middle distance running performance pubmed
strength training 5K running economy distance runners meta-analysis pubmed
explosive strength training distance running economy performance pubmed
coverage_notes: Strong coverage: 2 meta-analyses on strength training for runners (Llanos-Lagos 2024 performance + economy), 1 meta-analysis on HIIT/VO2max (Bacon 2013), 1 systematic review on strength training determinants (Blagrove 2018), plus 4 running-specific experimental studies covering SIT (Denham 2015), explosive strength (Paavolainen 1999), speed endurance (Skovgaard 2018), and neuromuscular factors (Nummela 2006). Good mix of foundational and recent papers.

papers.title: The Effect of Strength Training Methods on Middle-Distance and Long-Distance Runners' Athletic Performance: A Systematic Review with Meta-analysis
papers.notes: Systematically reviewed and meta-analyzed effects of different strength training methods on middle- and long-distance runners' performance including time trials and maximal speed.

papers.title: Effects of Strength Training on Running Economy in Highly Trained Runners: A Systematic Review with Meta-Analysis
papers.notes: Systematically reviewed and meta-analyzed effects of strength training on running economy in highly trained runners, comparing training modalities.

papers.title: VO2max trainability and high intensity interval training in humans: a meta-analysis
papers.notes: Meta-analyzed VO2max improvements from HIIT protocols, quantifying dose-response and effect sizes across training studies.

papers.title: Effects of Strength Training on the Physiological Determinants of Middle- and Long-Distance Running Performance: A Systematic Review
papers.notes: Systematic review examining effects of strength training on VO2max, running economy, body composition, and time trial performance in middle- and long-distance runners.

papers.title: Sprint interval training decreases circulating MicroRNAs important for muscle development
papers.journal: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
papers.notes: Examined effects of sprint interval training on 5km running performance and circulating microRNAs in recreational runners.

papers.title: Explosive-strength training improves 5-km running time by improving running economy and muscle power
papers.notes: Foundational study showing explosive strength training improved 5K time by 3% through enhanced running economy and neuromuscular characteristics, without changing VO2max.

papers.title: Concurrent speed endurance and resistance training improves performance, running economy, and muscle NHE1 in moderately trained runners
papers.notes: Examined how concurrent speed endurance and resistance training improved running performance and economy in moderately trained runners over 8 weeks.

papers.title: Neuromuscular factors determining 5 km running performance and running economy in well-trained athletes
papers.notes: Examined neuromuscular factors (muscle power, stiffness, pre-activation) as determinants of 5km performance and running economy in well-trained distance runners.
